name: Fanny Brice
abstract: Fania Borach (October 29, 1891 – May 29, 1951), known professionally as Fanny Brice or Fannie Brice, was an American comedian, illustrated song model, singer, and actress who made many stage, radio, and film appearances. She is known as the creator and star of the top-rated radio comedy series The Baby Snooks Show.

concerts.label: Artists Series of The Association of Music School Settlements

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Mondello
about_quotes.evidence: So you grow up hearing all of these Sophie Tucker tunes and Fanny Brice tunes and tunes from the 20s and whatever.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Fanny Brice’s trunk. Mae West’s trunk. Eva Tanguay’s trunks. W. C. Field’s trunks. All them trunks, they had a certain warehouse on Broadway that used to take these trunks.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Hannah, her sister, went into Buddy Rose's 'Crazy Quilt' with Fanny Brice... And I was in the pit. Playing in the pit.

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: "'My Man,' which was written for Fannie Brice, I think they said, was a musical prostitute."

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: He said, '...she is suddenly in a class with Ethel Waters, Fanny Brice, and Peggy Lee.'
